ratings.comment: He only reads from the slides and is super boring. We dont do any concrete exams in class and the notes are also useless.

I couldn't have had a worse introduction to the physics department at Madison. Once per class, he would mess up a formula by either switching variables or flipping addition and subtraction signs. He spent most classes deriving formulas and he always answered in-class questions in a way that a student at our levels just couldn't understand.

He does an okay job of explaining concepts on the board, and provides examples instead of just plowing through the equations. However, when writing on the board he will sometimes mix up his variables or negative signs, which can make it more confusing to understand how the math actually works.

Nice guy but hard to follow. Had trouble teaching lectures in a way that was understandable. Often had to backtrack and overall a bit monotone. Seemed to be very knowledgeable about physics but not a good match for an intro course.

Did his best to help with the grade/relieve pressure. Minimal homework, completion labs and generous curve.

Completely clueless professor. He has absolutely no idea how to lead a classroom, as a result his lectures are a complete waste of time. You're better off using your time with a tutor and reading the textbook.

Physics 202 is a challenging class most engineering majors cannot avoid at UW-Madison. Professor Herndon admittedly does present somewhat stale lectures, but presents useful equations and concepts during lecture. The textbook recommended is the holy-grail for 202; mastery of the conceptual and mathematical problems in the text will help immensely.
